reasoning: The question resolves YES if New People (NL) achieves the largest net seat gain in the 2026 Russian State Duma election. Current polling shows United Russia and the Communist Party (CPRF) losing support compared to 2021, meaning they will likely lose seats. The two main parties poised to gain seats are the Liberal Democratic Party of Russia (LDPR) and New People (NL). Polling indicates both could see ~40-50 seat gains. However, LDPR consistently edges out NL in polls (e.g., 13.3% vs 11.1%), and importantly, LDPR has historically secured single-mandate district seats while NL won zero in 2021. Additionally, the tiebreaker rules favor the party with more valid votes, heavily leaning toward LDPR based on current projections. Thus, LDPR is the clear favorite to gain the most seats, making NL a plausible but distinct underdog. The Polymarket price of 24.5% perfectly reflects these dynamics.
